explanations.37: This line uses Webpack's require.context to find all MDX files in the blog folder—it's a special build-time feature that bundles file references so we can load them dynamically later.
explanations.41: The .keys() method returns an array of file paths that matched our search pattern, so we can loop through each blog post file.
explanations.43: This actually loads and executes the MDX file, giving us back an object that contains the frontMatter (the metadata at the top of the file) plus any exported content.
explanations.45: This extracts the folder name by removing '/index.mdx' or '/index.md' from the end, then strips off the leading './' that Webpack adds—so 'blog/my-post/index.mdx' becomes just 'my-post'.
explanations.57: If the image path starts with './', that means it's a relative path from the post file, so we need to convert it to an absolute path the browser can actually load.
explanations.76: The .filter(Boolean) removes any falsy values (null, undefined, false) from the array, so invalid posts won't make it into the final list.
